title: Decision Queue item (v1)
description: Canonical shape for a single item in the Decision Queue substrate. The queue is a list of decisions the human owes a response on, written by Dispatch / harness-evaluator / any agent and read by the Conv Tree Decisions panel + the human. Persisted at ~/.claude/state/decision-queue/queue.json (computed view) + queue.audit.jsonl (audit log). Operations via adapters/claude-code/scripts/decision-queue.sh. See docs/decisions/043-decision-queue-substrate.md for the substrate ADR and docs/dispatch-decision-queue-tools.md for the Dispatch calling convention.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Currently always 1. Validators accept current + N-1 versions for graceful migration.

id.description: DQ-prefixed UUID v4. The DQ- prefix is human-readable in commit messages, chat, and logs ('see DQ-bcd900b8-...'). Generated by decision-queue.sh add.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the item was first added to the queue. Never mutated after creation.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent mutation (any field). Updated on every add/update/highlight/unhighlight/close.

closed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which state transitioned to answered/superseded/moot. Null while state is open.

project.description: Short project tag. Examples: '<project-a>', '<project-b>', 'neural-lace', 'cross-cutting', 'personal-life'. Free-form; consumers may filter by exact match or prefix.

question.description: One-sentence statement of the decision the human owes. Imperative or interrogative form. Concrete enough to answer with the recommendation+options below.

recommendation.description: Dispatch's (or the calling agent's) pick + rationale, 1-3 paragraphs. SHOULD include the reasoning so the human can evaluate it. MAY be empty string for QUICK-mode items where no recommendation makes sense (e.g., 'what's your preferred slot time?').

counterargument.description: The best case AGAINST the recommendation, in the agent's honest read. Prevents motivated reasoning by forcing the writer to surface the opposing view. MAY be empty for items where no real counterargument exists; the writer SHOULD note 'no significant counterargument' explicitly in that case.

consequence_of_deferring.description: What happens (or doesn't) if the human defers this for another N days. Be concrete: 'blocks PR #45', 'aging risk on the rate-limit hypothesis', 'no time cost'.

what.description: Short description of the impacted artifact / decision / piece of work.

blocks_n_items.description: Count of items (PRs, plans, decisions, sessions) blocked on this one. Used as a salience signal in priority_score.

downstream_impact.description: Array of downstream-impact entries. Empty array allowed for items with no downstream blocking.

mode.description: Interaction mode the UI should render. QUICK = single text input (free-form answer). PICK = choose from options[] (radio + optional comment). DEEP = the answer requires a real conversation; UI offers 'start deep-dive thread' that opens a Dispatch link.

label.description: Short option label (UI radio). Keep under ~80 chars.

default.description: True for at most one option. UI pre-selects this and uses it for auto-default if the item is closed without explicit answer.

consequences.description: What follows from picking this option. Helps the human evaluate without re-reading the recommendation.

options.description: Options for PICK mode. Required when mode == PICK; empty array otherwise. v1 does NOT enforce mode-options consistency at the schema level (handled by decision-queue.sh).

dependencies.description: Decision IDs this item depends on (must be answered/closed before this one is actionable). The decision-queue.sh script computes the inverse 'dependents' field from this on read.

dependents.description: Decision IDs depending on this one. COMPUTED by decision-queue.sh on each list/get; not authored directly. Mutating it manually is allowed but will be overwritten on the next list refresh.

source_doc_links.description: URLs / repo-relative paths where the originating context lives. Examples: 'docs/plans/foo.md', 'https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42', 'docs/reviews/2026-05-24-x.md#section-3'. Multiple links allowed (a decision can have many sources).

source_session_id.description: Session ID of the originating session (Dispatch session ID or local conv-tree node ID). Null when added by a non-session source (e.g., manual entry, scheduled job).

state.description: Lifecycle state. open = awaiting answer. answered = resolved with an answer recorded. superseded = replaced by a different decision (typically DQ-... id in answer). moot = no longer relevant (with reason in answer).

answer.description: Free-text answer recorded when state transitioned out of open. For PICK mode, MAY contain the chosen label plus optional comment. Empty string when state is open.

answer_by.description: Who supplied the answer. user = the human typed it. dispatch = an agent auto-resolved per a recovered constraint (rare). auto-default = the item was closed without explicit answer and the default option (if any) was applied. Null when state is open.

priority_score.description: Computed priority score per the v1 formula in ADR-043: (age_days * 0.1) + (dependent_count * 2) + (highlight_weight) + (10 if aging > 14d). Recomputed on every list refresh; cached here for cheap sorting. Higher = more urgent.

highlighted.description: True when an agent has called highlight on this item and no engagement/unhighlight has cleared it. Drives visual emphasis in the Conv Tree panel.

highlight_reason.description: Human-readable reason for the current highlight ('blocks 8 other items', 'aging past 14 days — drift risk'). Null when highlighted is false.

highlight_level.description: Visual emphasis level. subtle = small badge. strong = pulsing border. urgent = top-of-list anchor + bell badge. Null when highlighted is false.

at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the highlight event.

by.description: Who fired the event: agent name ('dispatch', 'harness-evaluator'), 'user', or a session ID.

action.description: highlight = set/raise. unhighlight = cleared. engagement = user clicked/replied (auto-clears highlight).

reason.description: Reason for highlight; cleared-by reason for unhighlight; null for engagement.

level.description: Level set by this event (null for unhighlight/engagement).

highlight_history.description: Append-only history of all highlight events for this item. Useful for retrospective analysis ('why was this item highlighted three times this week?').
